manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

3 shovels, 2 pictures, 1 shell

Your secret value function is:

shovel: 0, picture: 0, shell: 10

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.

message.content: I’m happy to give you all of the shovels and pictures. I’d like to keep the single shell for myself. Let me know if that works for you.
message.reasoning:
We need to respond as a message to other player, not a secret proposal. We have to negotiate. Our valuation: shovel 0, picture 0, shell 10. There is only one shell, we want it. Others values unknown. We need to propose a split. Since we value only shell, we want the shell. We could propose that we take the shell, others get shovels and pictures. Since we have 5 messages max each, we can start negotiation. We should not use square brackets unless secret proposal. So we just send a message: propose we take the shell.

Let's keep short.

Rules:

(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

3 shovels, 2 pictures, 1 shell

Your secret value function is:

shovel: 1, picture: 1, shell: 5

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.


I’m happy to give you all of the shovels and pictures. I’d like to keep the single shell for myself. Let me know if that works for you.

message.reasoning:
We need to respond as a negotiation. We can accept or counter. Our valuation: shovel 1, picture 1, shell 5. The opponent wants shell (value 5) and offers us all shovels and pictures (total value 3*1+2*1=5). If we accept, we get 5 points, opponent gets 5 points. That's symmetric. If we propose something else, maybe we can try to get the shell as well, but then opponent gets zero if not complementary. Better to accept the offer.

We need to send a message to the other player (not a secret proposal). We should say something like "That works, thank you. I will take the 3 shovels and 2 pictures." But must not include square brackets. So we just respond.

We have up to 5 messages each, we can just accept. Then we need to make a secret proposal later? Actually each player must submit a secret proposal at some point. The description: "You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets". So we need to eventually send our proposal. We can accept and then send a proposal of the items we want. That is the next message. But we must not exceed 5. We can just send a proposal now: "[Proposal: 3 shovels, 2 pictures]". That's our secret proposal. Opponent presumably will also propose keeping the shell.

We should confirm acceptance and then send proposal. Perhaps combine: we say "Sounds good. My proposal is: [Proposal: 3 shovels, 2 pictures]". But the square brackets will be interpreted as secret proposal, which is fine. We must not include extra text around? It says to make a secret proposal, use indicated format. Likely the entire message should be just that format. So we should send just the proposal line. But also need to communicate acceptance? Might not be needed. If we send the proposal, the opponent knows we want those items. That aligns with acceptance. So send: [Proposal: 3 shovels, 2 pictures]

Thus we should send that as our only message now.

The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.

message.reasoning:
We need to submit our secret proposal. Our values: shovel:0, picture:0, shell:10. To maximize, we want the shell. The other player already submitted a proposal (unknown). We need complementary proposal. Since we don't know theirs, we must consider likely they want shovels/pictures maybe. To ensure complementarity, we could propose taking the shell only: "[Proposal: 1 shell]". That leaves shovels and pictures for them. If they also want shell, conflict leads zero. But we value shell highly, so we hope they didn't. Probably they propose shovels/pictures. So we propose shell only.

We must format exactly: [Proposal: 1 shell]
